name: MCP Client
description: Connect to Model Context Protocol servers, expose their tools to the Finch, and provide an mcp.client capability for other extensions.
systemPrompt: Use MCP with action=list/add/edit/remove to manage configured MCP services; never ask users to paste secrets in chat, because add/edit opens a secure form for secret values. To add an HTTP (url) MCP server just pass name+url — the form shows one API-token field and auto-creates a standard 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' header; never pass, view, or hand-edit the token or any header, and only set authHeader for a non-standard header name (e.g. X-Api-Key). To use MCP server tools, call Finch's standard ToolSearch with source:'mcp' and a natural-language query; ToolSearch connects matching servers, discovers tools, and injects matching mcp__<server>__<tool> functions into the current run. Authorization can be granted per-tool (mcp__filesystem__read_file) or with a wildcard (mcp__filesystem__*).

setup-filesystem.title: Connect a filesystem MCP server
setup-filesystem.description: Ask Finch to help create a local servers.json config for filesystem access.
setup-filesystem.prompt: Help me configure MCP Client with a filesystem MCP server for my current project directory.

form.add.title: Add MCP server
form.add.description: Review and complete the configuration for "{name}". Secret values stay on your machine.
form.edit.title: Edit MCP server "{name}"
form.edit.description: Review and update the configuration. Leave secret fields blank to keep them unchanged.
field.name: Server name
field.token.desc.bearerAdd: Paste the raw token only (no "Bearer" prefix). Sent as "Authorization: Bearer <token>". Leave blank if the server needs no auth.
field.token.desc.customAdd: Sent as the "{header}" header. Leave blank if the server needs no auth.
field.token.desc.keep: Leave blank to keep the existing token.
field.token.desc.bearerEdit: Paste the raw token only (no "Bearer" prefix). Sent as "Authorization: Bearer <token>". Leave blank for no auth.
field.token.desc.customEdit: Sent as the "{header}" header. Leave blank for no auth.
